sn.s: Am Friitignamittag git's oft en Apéro.
sn.e: On Friday afternoons there's often an apéro.

sn.s: En Apéro isch es chliises Träffe mit öppis z'trinke.
sn.e: An apéro is a small gathering with something to drink.

sn.s: Es git Wii, Bier und chli öppis z'chnabbere.
sn.e: There's wine, beer and a little something to nibble.

sn.s: Chäs, Nüssli und Brötli ghöred dezue.
sn.e: Cheese, nuts and little bread rolls are part of it.

sn.s: "Chum doch au, mir mached en Apéro," het en Kolleg gseit.
sn.e: "Come along, we're having an apéro," a colleague said.

sn.s: Mer staht zäme, redet und lacht.
sn.e: You stand around together, chat and laugh.

sn.s: Bevor mer trinkt, stosst mer aa.
sn.e: Before you drink, you clink glasses.

sn.s: Debii luegt mer enand i d'Auge und seit: "Pröschtli!"
sn.e: While doing so you look each other in the eye and say: "Cheers!"

sn.s: Wer nöd i d'Auge luegt, het Päch, säged d'Lüt.
sn.e: Whoever doesn't look you in the eye has bad luck, people say.

sn.s: De Apéro isch gmüetlich und nöd hektisch.
sn.e: The apéro is cosy and not hectic.

sn.s: Mer het Ziit, und das gnüsst mer.
sn.e: You have time, and you enjoy that.

sn.s: So lärnt mer Kollege besser kenne als im Büro.
sn.e: That way you get to know colleagues better than in the office.

sn.s: Es isch gsellig, ohni dass mer's übertribt.
Es paar Glas, en guete Schwätz, und de Friitig isch perfekt.
sn.e: A few glasses, a good chat, and Friday is perfect.
